Comment:
Public Comment – Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP)
March 18–19, 2026 Meeting
Docket No. CDC–2026–0199

I am submitting this executive summary along with my full public comment letter, a peer-reviewed case study detailing my longitudinal laboratory findings, and a comprehensive list of ICD codes assigned since 2021. All referenced documentation is being uploaded with this submission.

I am a U.S. patient who developed progressive multi-system illness following three doses of the Pfizer–BioNTech COVID-19 mRNA vaccine. Diagnoses since 2021 have included pulmonary emboli, progressive bilateral sensorineural hearing loss with persistent tinnitus, chronic pancreatitis confirmed by endoscopic ultrasound, small intestine bacterial overgrowth, small fiber neuropathy, autonomic dysfunction, biopsy-confirmed dermatologic inflammatory disease, and MRI-confirmed myocarditis. These conditions have resulted in sustained functional impairment. I remain medically disabled and have been unable to return to my more than 20-year career in the medical industry.

For nearly two years, recurrent chest pain and exertional symptoms were attributed to anxiety until cardiac MRI documented myocarditis. That diagnosis prompted comprehensive evaluation across cardiology, immunology, rheumatology, infectious disease, neurology, and gastroenterology. Extensive investigation across infectious, autoimmune, genetic, metabolic, hematologic, and toxicologic domains did not identify an alternative unifying cause.

During evaluation for possible asymptomatic SARS-CoV-2 infection, routine serology revealed persistently elevated spike antibodies more than two years after vaccination, while nucleocapsid antibodies remained negative across independent laboratories. Testing was repeated to exclude laboratory error, with consistent results. Persistent antibody titers prompted further investigation.

Longitudinal molecular studies were subsequently performed across multiple independent laboratories using complementary methodologies. Findings included persistent spike protein in plasma and circulating exosomes beyond 1,100 days post-vaccination; RT-PCR detection of vaccine-derived spike mRNA sequences in exosomes beyond 1,200 days; detection of plasmid backbone elements within peripheral blood mononuclear cells; detection of spike protein and plasmid DNA sequences in serial skin biopsies beyond 1,300 days; immunohistochemical detection of spike protein within endothelial cells and perivascular macrophages; detection of spike protein and vaccine-associated sequences within PBMCs, including spike protein–positive monocytes; and ongoing immune dysregulation documented on serial cytokine and transcriptomic analysis.

This is a single case; therefore, the findings were gathered longitudinally, across independent laboratories, and using converging detection platforms to enhance reliability. Reproducible findings over time warrant structured replication rather than dismissal.

I am hesitant to characterize this phenomenon as rare because longitudinal human studies specifically designed to evaluate molecular persistence have not been conducted. Without systematic evaluation, frequency cannot be determined. Most citizens lack the financial resources or biological literacy to pursue multi-year molecular investigation. If persistence occurs in individuals, current safety monitoring frameworks are not structured to detect it.

Recent investigative reporting and congressional findings have examined internal federal discussions regarding communication of myocarditis risk signals during vaccine rollout, indicating that concerns about vaccine hesitancy influenced timing and framing of public safety communication. As someone later diagnosed with MRI-confirmed myocarditis after prolonged attribution of symptoms to anxiety, transparent and timely safety communication is critically important.

ACIP plays a central advisory role in shaping national vaccine policy and post-authorization safety priorities. I respectfully request that ACIP support longitudinal human biodistribution and persistence studies; independent audit and transparent reporting of residual plasmid DNA levels and fragment size distribution; development of structured evaluation pathways for patients with persistent laboratory anomalies following vaccination; and review of compensation and surveillance frameworks to better account for delayed or progressive diagnoses.

Scientific integrity requires structured replication of converging longitudinal findings. Transparency and rigorous investigation are essential to maintaining durable public confidence.

Respectfully submitted.- Steve

My first statement might surprise you - I am pro vaccines.  I think they can save lives.  Unfortunately, they can also negatively impact lives permanently.  I am one of those people.  I have no spleen as a result of a bad car accident decades ago in which I suffered internal injures, including rupture of my spleen, which resulted in me being immunocompromised.  "No big deal" as long as I took precautions, according to my doctors.  That's because I could still have vaccines.  Then along came COVID.  I had my first dose of the vaccine.  I was shocked to hear people complain that they had a little aching.  A "little aching?"  I had a high fever and my muscles hurt so badly I could barely raise myself from my couch.  Little did I know that was just the beginning of my reactions.  The next dose was worse.  And with the third, I thought I was having a flare of my mild small fiber neuropathy.  Except it was different, unlike anything I had ever encountered before.  Both hands and both feet were tingling and numb.  And then the sensation began to spread up my legs.  I was trying to not panic, telling myself "something" had aggravated a flare of my neuropathy and to just hang in since there wasn't really anything my doctors could do for my neuropathy.  And then my right eyelid drooped and closed.  And I couldn't open it.  First I went to my optometrist because I already had a scheduled appointment and could get in quickly.  He sent me to an ophthalmologist who specialized in eyelids.  By the time I got in, I was better, but when his fellow had me look around, my eyelid again became droopy.  The ophthalmologist thought I had myasthenia gravis.  Tests were negative.  I told my primary physician, who said I should call my neurologist right away.  After seeing his fellow, my neurologist came in and immediately asked me if I had had any vaccines.  Yes, maybe a week or two before symptoms started.  He told me I had had Guillain Barre Syndrome (GBS) and said I should recover.  The next fall, my primary told me she was concerned about me not getting vaccines because of my compromised immune system and hardly anyone ever had GBS again - please get the flu vaccine.   I did.  Around a week or so later, the same thing happened - tinging and numbness in my feet and hands, moving up my legs, and drooping of my eyelid.  The verdict from my immunologist was that it was not safe for me to have more vaccines because now my body overreacts to them.  Initially (and still) I thought about the dangers of someone with a compromised immune system being unable to have vaccines again.  My immunologist has reinforced that because she has told me that it's not just COVID that could kill me - it's the flu, RSV, and more.  And she instructed me to wear a mask whenever I am away from home.  But that's only part of how the vaccine caused me to react has affected my life.  As you know, socialization in America revolves around food.  You can't eat food with a mask on.  My social life has almost entirely disappeared, as have my friends.  I guess it's too much trouble to call or even message someone they can't meet in person over a meal.  Three times a year, I literally risk death by celebrating Thanksgiving, Christmas, and Easter without a mask.  I do the same for a few birthdays.  I go and hide that I am wondering if I will get sick, if I will survive, because my doctors have repeatedly told me that just "avoiding sick people" isn't enough because someone can be contagious for two full days before they even know they're sick.  My life will never be the same.  Frankly, I will never consider myself worthy of love, worthy of caring, worthy of friendship again because of the people that have disappeared from my life.  And yet, the manufacturers have not been held accountable for what happened to me or others.  Please hold them accountable for the few lives lost and the more lives that were forever changed.  Because, although we are few, we DO matter.

My name is Jeremy, I am 52 years old, and I experienced an adverse reaction to my COVID-19 vaccination. I received the J&J shot on April 9th, 2021, lot #205A21A.

My life changed in that moment. My body spiraled into a horrific state, and I spent the next several years searching for answers, depleting my finances, and left with nearly nobody willing or able to help.

My symptoms included swollen lymph nodes, uncontrollable convulsions, autonomic dysfunction, and the constant feeling that my entire body was under siege. At the time, COVID-19 vaccination was a political hot point, and doctors were either dismissive of my symptoms or unwilling to get involved when they saw the severity of my condition. I was passed around with no real support from the medical community. My only salvation was the community of others going through similar experiences.

I am fortunate in that I “only” lost three years of my life and nearly all my savings before I began to heal. I know many others have not been so lucky.

The best explanation I received after my neurological tests began showing abnormalities was that my shot triggered my immune system into a hyper-aggressive state. It didn’t know how to respond to what was introduced, and in doing so, it attacked anything it perceived as a threat, including nerve cells.

I initially wondered whether my reaction might have been linked to a prior COVID infection, with the shot acting as a trigger. Later, I was contacted by the Baltimore Sun regarding a J&J manufacturing plant. Apparently, my lot was first sent to England but rejected due to a high percentage of adverse reactions. Instead of marking the batch as contaminated, J&J renumbered it and distributed it in the U.S.

As unjust as my situation has been, I am sharing my story to give voice to those vaccine-injured individuals who weren’t as fortunate. Nerve cells can heal with time, but I know many have suffered far more severe and permanent reactions. They need help and someone willing to listen.

Thank you for your time.
Jeremy

I am submitting this comment to request clarification regarding the evidentiary standards used in the recommendation of mRNA COVID-19 products.

I am also an individual who experienced serious adverse effects following COVID-19 vaccination. Within approximately 24 hours of receiving the injection, I experienced vision loss in one eye. I also experienced hearing loss in both ears and medical findings including brain swelling and lesions that required ongoing evaluation and treatment. As someone directly affected by the standards used to mandate these products, I respectfully seek clarification on the evidentiary and risk-benefit frameworks that informed these policy decisions. I am still producing spike protein 5 years later.

Definition of "Vaccine" and Prevention Claims

At the time of initial recommendations, public messaging described these products as vaccines that would "prevent COVID-19." However, sterilizing immunity and transmission data were not established prior to broad recommendation.

What evidentiary standard did ACIP apply when determining that these products satisfied the criteria for a vaccine intended to prevent disease?

Was prevention defined as:

Prevention of infection?
Prevention of transmission?

If the definition of prevention evolved over time, when was that change formally reviewed and documented within ACIP deliberations? There were already approved products available for "treatment".

Additionally, public definitions of the term "vaccine" used in public health communications appear to have changed during the pandemic. If definitional language used in guidance or public materials changed during the period in which these products were recommended, when was that change formally reviewed and documented, and how did it affect the evidentiary criteria used in ACIP's evaluation of these products?

Risk Disclosure and Risk-Benefit Analysis

Risk-benefit determinations require transparent disclosure of known and potential risks.

At the time of emergency authorization and subsequent recommendation:

What long-term biodistribution or persistence data were available?
What manufacturing impurity thresholds were independently verified?
What adverse event signal thresholds were considered sufficient to reassess recommendations?
How did ACIP evaluate projected population-level benefits relative to uncertain long-term biological risks when recommending these products for healthy and lower-risk populations, including young adults?

Additionally and most importantly, how were potential risks involving genomic interaction or cellular effects evaluated in the risk-benefit analysis, and what level of uncertainty was considered acceptable when recommending these products broadly across the population?

Given that these recommendations were adopted by many institutions as the basis for vaccine mandates affecting employment, education, and participation in public life, how did ACIP evaluate the relationship between evolving safety data and the ability of individuals to provide meaningful informed consent?

Specifically:

What threshold of safety certainty was considered sufficient before recommending policies that effectively required vaccination for otherwise healthy individuals?

How were potential irreversible harms weighed in the risk-benefit analysis when long-term safety data were still evolving?

What mechanisms were in place to ensure individuals could provide meaningful informed consent if the full risk profile was still developing?

Regulatory Consistency

The FDA's guidance on gene therapy defines such products to include those utilizing "in vitro transcribed RNA."

Given that mRNA COVID-19 products are manufactured through in vitro transcription, has ACIP evaluated whether regulatory classification affects:

Long-term monitoring standards
Informed consent language
Safety surveillance duration

Notably, manufacturers of mRNA COVID-19 products describe their platform technology as gene therapy in public filings and disclosures to investors. Were such statements evaluated when determining the appropriate regulatory classification and monitoring framework for these products?

If regulatory definitions or classification assumptions differ between regulatory guidance, manufacturer disclosures, and public health policy, what mechanisms ensure that recommendations remain aligned with statutory definitions and established regulatory frameworks?

Public trust depends on definitional clarity and transparent evidentiary standards. I respectfully request that ACIP address these questions on the public record.

Because individuals like myself have experienced severe and lasting medical consequences following these recommendations, clear articulation of the evidentiary standards, risk-benefit thresholds, and definitions applied in these decisions is essential to restoring public trust and ensuring that future policy decisions are grounded in transparent scientific and regulatory reasoning.

Public Comment for the March 18, 2026, CDC ACIP Committee Meeting

To the CDC Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices:

I have no conflicts of interest to disclose.

You must reinstate unhindered universal COVID vaccine recommendations for people of all ages, at least once a year, for all COVID vaccine formulations. COVID vaccination reduces the risk of Long COVID, severe acute disease, disability, and death for people of all ages [1-9]. Vaccination within the last year with formulations updated to match the latest circulating COVID strains is needed for the best protection.

Vaccine recommendation frameworks must include strong ethical foundations and must make advances in disease prevention available to all. Denying access to safe and effective vaccines and treatments under the guise of research is a practice that has been recognized as unethical and unacceptable for decades. Thankfully, officials in Guinea-Bissau have stopped a harmful CDC-funded trial that would have denied access to the birth dose of the hepatitis B vaccine for thousands of babies [10].

This committee must recommend all safe and effective vaccines to ensure broad access at pharmacies, insurance coverage, and coverage for uninsured children through the Vaccines for Children program. Hepatitis B, HPV, MMR, MMRV, RSV, and other vaccines are essential to prevent unnecessary infections, harmful consequences of infections, and deaths of both children and adults. Vaccine access should not depend on what state you live in, and no one should die because of a lack of access to affordable vaccines.

Dear Members of the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices,

We hope this message finds you well. We wanted to share a letter from Dr. Siobhan Dunnavant, a board-certified OB-GYN with over 25 years in practice, former state legislator, and Senior Advisor to Advocates for Healthy Kids.

Dr. Dunnavant's letter emphasizes the importance of preserving access to childhood vaccines and maintaining an approach centered on shared decision-making between parents and their clinicians. The focus is on empowering individuals to make informed decisions in consultation with their healthcare providers.

As you prepare for the upcoming ACIP meeting, we thought these perspectives might be valuable for your consideration. If Dr. Dunnavant can serve as a resource or be helpful in any dialogue on these topics, please don't hesitate to let us know.

Thank you for your continued dedication to evidence-informed guidance that keeps access at the forefront.

Best regards,
Advocates for Healthy Kids

To the CDC Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices:

I have no conflicts of interest to disclose. I am a member of the steering committee for Wisconsin Families for Vaccines, and this comment is not intended to reflect the views of any group.

As a physician-scientist and public health advocate, I urge you to reinstate unhindered universal COVID vaccine recommendations for people of all ages, at least once a year, for all COVID vaccine formulations. COVID vaccination reduces the risk of Long COVID, severe acute disease, disability, and death for people of all ages [1-9]. Vaccination within the last year with formulations updated to match the latest circulating COVID strains is needed for the best protection.

Vaccine recommendation frameworks must include strong ethical foundations and make advances in disease prevention available to all. Frameworks that require placebo-controlled trials where existing safe and effective vaccines are available are unacceptable. Thankfully, officials in Guinea-Bissau have stopped a harmful CDC-funded trial that would have denied access to the birth dose of the hepatitis B vaccine for thousands of babies [10]. Denying access to safe and effective vaccines and treatments under the guise of research is a practice that has been recognized as unethical and unacceptable for more than half a decade. Anyone who wishes to deny basic human rights has no business being involved in developing public health policies.

This committee must recommend all safe and effective vaccines to ensure broad access at pharmacies, insurance coverage, and coverage for uninsured children through the Vaccines for Children program. Hepatitis B, MMR, MMRV, RSV, HPV, and other vaccines are essential to prevent unnecessary infections, harmful consequences of infections, and deaths of both children and adults. No one should die because of a lack of access to affordable vaccines.

Sincerely,
Kaitlin Sundling, MD, PhD

Vaccine harm-reduction protocols exist, they should be standards of practice.

Guidance from CDC currently recommends that aspiration of IM injections is unnecessary; that the potential for accidental intravenous injection is very small and that injection aspiration contributes to patient discomfort, which adds to over-all vaccine hesitancy. Christine M. Thomas shows in 'Blood Aspiration
During IM Injection(2015)' that nurses who practiced when aspiration of IM injections was the standard often remember aspirating blood at least once (with some as many as thirteen times). How was it decided that potential benefits of aspirating IM injections are outweighed by vaccine hesitancy?

Further; multiple vaccine-allergy screening protocols exist. An example is attached ('Dermal testing of vaccines for children at high risk of allergies, 2007' by Sugai et al). We know anaphylaxis is associated with potential severe morbidities and has an overall survival rate of less than 100% even in emergency setting, so why is vaccine-allergy screening offered only on patient request, in most cases?

These safety measures are basically free, requiring some additional time from the health care professional. Aspirating IM injections adds seconds, maybe minutes to the process of vaccination. In the case of Covid-19, vaccine recipients waited 15 minutes to see if they were going to be OK after being injected, they could have spent that time in the process of an allergy screening protocol *before* getting the vaccine. Vulnerable populations would benefit especially from making these harm-reduction strategies standards of practice.

regards,

Charlie

As a pediatrician, I have spent years following the ACIP workgroup reports and the ACIP deliberations. When the GRADE system was introduced, there was a distinct improvement in the quality of the discussions by the committee because the studies that had greater power, strong biostatistics, and biological plausibility were weighted more heavily than preprints and other publications that had poor study design, subpar logical frameworks, and discrepancies in their biostatistics or conclusions.

When the Evidence to Recommendation system was introduced, the committee participants were able to have strong action-oriented discussions that elevated their own understanding of the basic science, the clinical applications, the benefits to specific populations, and the individual and societal costs of the different options.

Not only did these systems help ACIP make easily defendable recommendations, the process was transparent and enabled anyone from a parent to a scientist to follow to the logical conclusion. For a pediatrician like me, who has always tried to elicit questions and concerns from the parents of my patients, the systems were great ways to explain the vaccine decisions to individual parents, groups of parents, or groups of physicians.

Abandoning the processes are what disappointed me the most about the new ACIP. In their first meeting, some of the committee members did not know what the vote was for because EtR wasn't in place. In other meetings, studies that were basically case reports were weighted more heavily than well-designed large studies and that would not have happened with GRADE.

This is an even more important issue when so many of the ACIP members do not have a background in immunology, infectious disease, pharmacology, epidemiology, toxicology, and physiology. Past groups benefitted from the structure of GRADE and EtR that evened the playing field without disregarding science.

It is difficult to rely on the recommendations in 2026 when these processes, which themselves took years to develop and were instituted after rigorous analysis and discussion, were thrown out and no structured processes took their place. The public needs ACIP to return to a strong process-driven meeting agenda with discussions informed by the CDC professional staff and public comment.

Our health is too important to have eliminated the infrastructure of sound scientific and public health decision making.

My name is Jamie Schanbaum, and I am a survivor of meningococcal meningitis.

Seventeen years ago, meningitis changed my life forever. Within hours of my first symptoms, I woke up in the hospital, intubated and fighting for my life. I spent seven months hospitalized and ultimately lost all four of my limbs. Today, I live as a quad amputee.

Living this life is not easy. From something as simple as a handshake introduction to feeding myself, bathing, and navigating everyday social situations, every task requires adaptation and determination. It is a life that demands resilience every single day.

What makes my story especially difficult is that it was preventable.

I was a student at my dream university when I got sick. College students are at a higher risk for meningococcal disease(meningitis), which is exactly why vaccination is so important. I had not even completed my first semester when meningitis struck.

No one should have to experience watching their body deteriorate from a disease that we have the tools to prevent.

Today, 17 years later, I am finally living what feels like my best life. I am an advocate, an athlete, and someone who works every day to educate others about the importance of vaccination so that families never have to experience what mine did.

My outcome was considered “lucky.” Many people do not survive meningococcal disease at all.

I urge ACIP to continue recommending meningococcal vaccines on the immunization schedule and to protect access to these lifesaving vaccines. Vaccines prevent suffering, disability, and death. They protect students, families, and communities.

Please keep meningococcal vaccination recommendations strong and continue prioritizing prevention so that no one else has to endure this devastating disease.

Respectfully,
Jamie Schanbaum
Meningitis Survivor and Vaccine Advocate

My prior comment had an error that I did not catch, it stated my case study was peer reviewed. It should have stated my case study has been submitted for peer review. Here is the correction:
Public Comment – Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP)
March 18–19, 2026 Meeting
Docket No. CDC–2026–0199
I am submitting this executive summary along with my full public comment letter, a case study submitted for peer-review (pending) detailing my longitudinal laboratory findings, and a comprehensive list of ICD codes assigned since 2021. All referenced documentation is being uploaded with this submission.
I am a U.S. patient who developed progressive multi-system illness following three doses of the Pfizer–BioNTech COVID-19 mRNA vaccine. Diagnoses since 2021 have included pulmonary emboli, progressive bilateral sensorineural hearing loss with persistent tinnitus, chronic pancreatitis confirmed by endoscopic ultrasound, small intestine bacterial overgrowth, small fiber neuropathy, autonomic dysfunction, biopsy-confirmed dermatologic inflammatory disease, and MRI-confirmed myocarditis. These conditions have resulted in sustained functional impairment. I remain medically disabled and have been unable to return to my more than 20-year career in the medical industry.
For nearly two years, recurrent chest pain and exertional symptoms were attributed to anxiety until cardiac MRI documented myocarditis. That diagnosis prompted comprehensive evaluation across cardiology, immunology, rheumatology, infectious disease, neurology, and gastroenterology. Extensive investigation across infectious, autoimmune, genetic, metabolic, hematologic, and toxicologic domains did not identify an alternative unifying cause.
During evaluation for possible asymptomatic SARS-CoV-2 infection, routine serology revealed persistently elevated spike antibodies more than two years after vaccination, while nucleocapsid antibodies remained negative across independent laboratories. Testing was repeated to exclude laboratory error, with consistent results. Persistent antibody titers prompted further investigation.
Longitudinal molecular studies were subsequently performed across multiple independent laboratories using complementary methodologies. Findings included persistent spike protein in plasma and circulating exosomes beyond 1,100 days post-vaccination; RT-PCR detection of vaccine-derived spike mRNA sequences in exosomes beyond 1,200 days; detection of plasmid backbone elements within peripheral blood mononuclear cells; detection of spike protein and plasmid DNA sequences in serial skin biopsies beyond 1,300 days; immunohistochemical detection of spike protein within endothelial cells and perivascular macrophages; detection of spike protein and vaccine-associated sequences within PBMCs, including spike protein–positive monocytes; and ongoing immune dysregulation documented on serial cytokine and transcriptomic analysis.
This is a single case; therefore, the findings were gathered longitudinally, across independent laboratories, and using converging detection platforms to enhance reliability. Reproducible findings over time warrant structured replication rather than dismissal.
I am hesitant to characterize this phenomenon as rare because longitudinal human studies specifically designed to evaluate molecular persistence have not been conducted. Without systematic evaluation, frequency cannot be determined. Most citizens lack the financial resources or biological literacy to pursue multi-year molecular investigation. If persistence occurs in individuals, current safety monitoring frameworks are not structured to detect it.
Recent investigative reporting and congressional findings have examined internal federal discussions regarding communication of myocarditis risk signals during vaccine rollout, indicating that concerns about vaccine hesitancy influenced timing and framing of public safety communication. As someone later diagnosed with MRI-confirmed myocarditis after prolonged attribution of symptoms to anxiety, transparent and timely safety communication is critically important.
ACIP plays a central advisory role in shaping national vaccine policy and post-authorization safety priorities. I respectfully request that ACIP support longitudinal human biodistribution and persistence studies; independent audit and transparent reporting of residual plasmid DNA levels and fragment size distribution; development of structured evaluation pathways for patients with persistent laboratory anomalies following vaccination; and review of compensation and surveillance frameworks to better account for delayed or progressive diagnoses.
Scientific integrity requires structured replication of converging longitudinal findings. Transparency and rigorous investigation are essential to maintaining durable public confidence.
Respectfully submitted.

Steve K

My name is Kenneth Carmody. I am asking for your attention — and your conscience.

On July 21st, 2021, I received the Moderna COVID-19 vaccine in Los Angeles, California. Immediately afterward, I felt something was deeply wrong. What followed has been over three years of relentless, progressive physical collapse.

Before that day, I had visited over 100 countries. I ran and walked for miles through nature. I was a person fully alive in the world. That person is gone.

**My documented diagnoses since vaccination include:**

- Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis (ALS)
- Postural Tachycardia Syndrome (POTS) and autonomic dysregulation
- Mast Cell Activation Syndrome (MCAS) with multiple allergies and histamine intolerance
- Mitochondriopathy with profound fatigue
- Sensory and autonomic small-fiber neuropathies (whole-body distribution)
- Endothelial inflammation and vascular dysregulation
- Chronic neuropathic pain — trigeminal and occipital neuralgia
- Severe dysphagia (swallowing disorder); partially paralyzed diaphragm
- Persistent spike protein reactivity (“spikeopathy”), confirmed by laboratory markers
- Non-specific demyelination-like white matter lesions on MRI
- Severe microbiome dysbiosis: near-absent bifidobacterial populations, increased intestinal permeability, recurrent functional dyspepsia, and evidence of chronic pancreatitis

Daily, I experience episodes mimicking ischemic strokes — esophageal and laryngeal spasms, hypercranial tension, sinus swelling — that force me flat, fighting to calm my own body. This happens three to four times a day. Every day.

Since the end of 2023, I am no longer medically transportable. I cannot leave my immediate area. I have not traveled beyond a quarter mile from my home in 18 months. I am mostly bedridden. Outpatient appointments, hospital stays, and standardized diagnostics are physically impossible for me. I cannot work, drive, grocery shop, or reliably care for myself. Because of this, I am forced to advocate for my own case — from my bed — because no system has stepped in to help.

And I want you to understand — I am not alone in this.

There are thousands like me: uncounted, dismissed, and deteriorating. We go untracked because no federal body has committed to rigorously counting us. We go untreated because physicians fear professional consequences for acknowledging our condition. We go uncompensated because the PREP Act strips us of all legal recourse. The Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program has denied the vast majority of COVID vaccine injury claims.

I am here with four specific requests:

1. Acknowledge us. Formally recognize post-vaccination injury as a legitimate, serious adverse event category deserving clinical recognition.

2. Study us. Fund urgent, independent research into the mechanisms, prevalence, and treatment of COVID vaccine injuries — with the same resources given to vaccine development.

3. Care for us. Build a coordinated medical infrastructure for the injured — physicians trained and protected to treat us, and remote diagnostic capacity for those, like me, who cannot leave home.

4. Make us whole. Reform or remove the liability shields that block injured Americans from legal recourse. Manufacturers must bear accountability. The 1986 National Childhood Vaccine Injury Act created a compensation framework — an equivalent must exist for COVID vaccine injuries.

I followed the guidance. I trusted these institutions. I am asking you to honor that trust.

Please count us. Study us. Treat us. Give us justice.

I am asking the CDC and ACIP to also consider the Americans injured overseas. We exist. We are citizens. We deserve the same recognition and pathway to care as those who are able to remain on American soil.

Thank you.

Kenny Carmody

My name is Jay Alberino. I am a 40-year-old business owner, husband, and father of two young daughters. I am submitting this comment while receiving treatment in Tokyo, Japan — at my own expense — for injuries I believe are directly related to COVID-19 vaccination, treatment that does not exist in the United States.
Following vaccination I developed Stage 3.5/4 amyloid fibrin microclots, May-Thurner syndrome with >50% left iliac vein compression, small fiber neuropathy, dysautonomia/POTS, and mast cell activation syndrome. My symptoms include episodic neurological events affecting the brainstem, head, neck and tongue, severe blood pressure fluctuations, and autonomic dysfunction that has profoundly impacted my ability to work and be present for my family. I have spent over $100,000 out of pocket. I have been dismissed by multiple conventional physicians. Insurance covers none of the treatments showing promise for my condition.
The core pathology driving my illness — amyloid fibrin microclots — is not recognized by physicians at Mass General Hospital, one of the world's most prestigious institutions. Despite substantial peer-reviewed research from Dr. Resia Pretorius and others documenting microclot prevalence in COVID- and vaccine-injured patients, diagnostic testing barely exists in the U.S. and is absent from standard clinical practice. I was forced to pursue specialized microscopy outside conventional channels simply to confirm what was happening in my own blood.
Most critically: this entire disease pathway originates in the microvasculature. Endothelial dysfunction is the upstream event driving microclot formation, small fiber neuropathy, autonomic dysfunction, and mast cell activation. These are not separate conditions — they are a cascade from microvascular injury that current diagnostics are not designed to detect. Had endothelial dysfunction been recognized and treated at symptom onset, downstream damage may have been avoided. Instead, I progressed through every stage of this illness while being told my labs were normal. This is a catastrophic failure of medical infrastructure for an injury pattern affecting millions of Americans.
I am currently undergoing Double Filtration Plasmapheresis (DFPP) and Stem Cell Growth Factor therapy at Edogawa Hospital in Tokyo — a protocol with emerging evidence for clearing amyloid fibrin microclots. This treatment does not exist in the United States. I paid for international flights, accommodation, and all medical costs out of pocket. I have completely depleted my savings. I am separated from my wife and daughters for weeks at a time. I have no other option.
I am not anti-vaccine. I am a patient who trusted the system, followed recommendations, and was left behind when something went wrong.
I urge ACIP to take the following actions:
1. Formally recognize post-vaccine injury as a distinct clinical syndrome requiring dedicated research, diagnostic criteria, and treatment protocols — equivalent to post-COVID recognition. Specifically, fund accessible microclot diagnostic testing and establish clinical guidelines for its use in post-vaccine and post-COVID patients.
2. Mandate microvascular and endothelial dysfunction screening at major academic medical centers so that injured patients are identified at the earliest stage — before the cascade of downstream damage becomes irreversible.
3. Establish a meaningful compensation pathway for vaccine-injured patients. The current VICP system is functionally inaccessible for conditions not yet formally recognized and imposes near-impossible burdens of proof.
4. Mandate insurance coverage for evidence-based treatments including plasmapheresis, autonomic rehabilitation, and fibrinolytic protocols — so Americans do not have to travel to Japan and spend their life savings to recover.
5. Fund domestic clinical trials for DFPP and related apheresis protocols currently available only abroad, so future patients are not forced to choose between bankruptcy and recovery.
There are thousands of people like me — documented, suffering, and invisible to the system that injured them. We are parents, spouses, and workers who want our lives back. We trusted public health institutions. We followed recommendations. We deserved better.
I respectfully ask this committee to see us, and to act.
Jay Alberino
Marblehead, Massachusetts

My name is Susan and my sister's name is Leanne. We are writing in response to Docket No. CDC-2026-0199. We appreciate this opportunity to share our mother's story and hope that it serves to shape how policies on vaccine recommendation guidelines, post-adverse event care, and research priorities.

Our mother died on June 2, 2022, just 16 days after receiving her 2nd Pfizer COVID-19 booster shot. Eight of those days were spent in the ICU. The death certificate lists pneumonia as the cause of death for our mom, but the doctor explained that there really isn't an ICD-10 diagnosis code for what caused her death; it was an averse immune response to her 2nd COVID-19 booster shot, which he was able to include in 'other contributing conditions' on the death certificate.

Our mom felt ill, like many people, the day after the shot, but didn't bounce back, like most people; she steadily grew worse and worse. On day three, she said she hadn't felt well since the injection. She had increasing difficulty breathing a week later and I brought her to the emergency room. She was admitted to the hospital due to her need for oxygen and abnormal pulse, moved to ICU the following day due to increasing needs for oxygen, BiPap and eventually requiring a ventilator after a week in the ICU. She had a heart attack after a day on the ventilator, and we knew she wasn't going to pull through; she died shortly after the device was removed. While on the ventilator, secretions taken from her lungs appeared to have bloody lung tissue in them. She was tested for COVID-19 multiple times while in the hospital because her lungs had the COVID-19 signature appearance to the doctors. The tests returned negative. She was tested for various other atypical pneumonias via Mayo Clinic lab, but all those tests were negative as well. She didn't have fungal or bacterial pneumonia; her white blood cell count was not elevated, and antibiotics and steroid treatments did not help. She steadily grew worse with increasing needs for oxygen. Upon final evaluation of all information, the pulmonologists deduced that she had experienced an adverse immune response to the COVID-19 booster shot.

I reported my mom's death in Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System (VAERS); the hospital where she died didn't report it, despite the last line of one of the pulmonologist's chart notes, "This should be reported as a potential adverse event of booster." A different pulmonologist cited it on her death certificate. Anyone would surely assume these mandated medical professionals would report it for tracking purposes, like they did all COVID-related deaths? Not so. My report in VAERS is the only report for my mom. I am a data analyst by profession, so I have queried the VAERS database with various filters - as few as possible, so as to catch the hospital's report of my mom's death. It just isn't there. When I looked through all the possible matches to my mom's VAERS report, I noticed a trend... Most of them appeared to be reported by either family members or skilled nursing facilities; none by hospitals. Herein lies the problem with a passive reporting system, like VAERS: we don't know what we don't know. Start by requiring hospitals to document the answer to the question, "When was your last vaccination?" Science without data is NOT science!

I have submitted a claim to the CICP, which was denied about this time last year. The primary reason for denial is that her doctors did not cite medical journal articles or cases when making their determination that the COVID-19 booster shot caused her death. Since when do doctors have time to do such research while caring for patients in the ICU? In the process of writing a response, requesting secondary review of the CICP claim, I researched medical journals and found plenty of cases like my mom's, reported by doctors in reputable journals. We are still awaiting a response from the CICP to that letter, and they say it may take years before we do.

If only our mom had known about the risk of death from getting her 2nd COVID-19 booster shot at her annual well visit on May 17, 2022 (and she was VERY well - lab reports from that day prove it) she may not have taken that second booster. She may have anyway. However, she deserved to have some informed consent. We all deserve that. Our lives, and our deaths, ought to count for something. I want her story to be told so that others will know about the risk of injury and death associated with COVID-19 vaccinations and continued COVID-19 boosters. If we are to "Be Informed!", as our mother's final Medicare EOB stated, urging members to keep themselves and others 'safe' by getting their COVID-19 booster shot, don't we need data to inform us? But what if the data isn't even collected? We urge the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices to consider this and other real-world experiences in its deliberations.

Public Comment for Docket No. CDC-2026-0199
My name is Dani Landers. I am writing in response to Docket No. CDC-2026-0199.
I am one of the people living in the space that is rarely acknowledged in public discussions about COVID vaccination. I live with a Covid vaccine injury from Moderna shots.
Since my vaccination, my health has changed in ways I never imagined. I developed significant neurological symptoms and Postural Orthostatic Tachycardia Syndrome (POTS), a form of autonomic dysfunction. The autonomic nervous system controls many essential bodily functions, and when it is disrupted, daily life becomes unpredictable. Standing, concentrating, regulating heart rate, and maintaining energy are no longer automatic. My body does not behave the way it once did.
The hardest part is living in the in-between. I want to remain a functional part of the world. I want to work, contribute, and participate in life the way I once did. Instead, I now live with limits that did not exist before.
On some days, the simplest activities become decisions about survival of energy. I have had mornings where I must choose between taking a shower or making a meal because my body may not have the energy to do both. These are the invisible trade-offs people living with autonomic dysfunction face every day.
I know I am not alone. Across patient communities, many individuals are reporting neurological complications and a significant rise in conditions such as POTS and other forms of autonomic dysfunction. These people deserve recognition, research, and support.
Vaccination has long been presented as part of a social contract. Individuals accept a small personal risk in order to contribute to the greater public good. If society asks people to take that risk, then society also carries a responsibility to care for those rare individuals who experience harm. That support should not be controversial. It should be a basic part of ethical public health policy.
I respectfully ask policymakers and the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices to consider the following:
• Recognition and continued research into neurological and autonomic complications following Covid vaccination
• Full transparency and public access to all vaccine safety data
• Reconsideration of liability protections that shield manufacturers from accountability
• Meaningful reform of the vaccine injury compensation system so it is fair, responsive, and accessible. The financial burden is crushing.
• The ability for individuals whose claims were denied, dismissed, or never filed due to barriers in the current system to resubmit claims under a revised and equitable program
Acknowledging vaccine injury does not weaken public health. Transparency, compassion, and accountability strengthen trust.
Public health should never require someone to disappear in order for a policy to succeed.
Respectfully,
Dani Landers

The authors of the paper, said their findings suggest the need for further research.

The paper identified mechanisms — including the spike protein and DNA contamination found in some COVID-19 vaccine types — that might be responsible for triggering cancer. The DNA contamination issue is something the vaccine manufacturers caused by gross negligence. There were also several "recurrent" themes in the studies they examined. These included “unusually rapid progression, recurrence, or reactivation” of preexisting conditions, the “atypical” appearance of cancers near the point of vaccination, and the reactivation of dormant tumors.

Several studies emphasized “unexpectedly rapid progression, atypical presentations, or unusually aggressive courses of disease.”

Forty-one percent of the studies identified a link between the COVID-19 vaccines and solid tumors, including melanoma, breast cancer, lung cancer, sarcomas and “organ-specific carcinomas, such as pancreatic cancer.”

Several studies “described unusually rapid onset, short-latency recurrence, or aggressive clinical progression” for these tumor types. Some of the studies “described tumor formation or recurrence at or near vaccine injection sites.”

The review cited several recent large-scale studies, including a two-year study of 8.4 million South Koreans published last year The South Korean study found a statistically significant link between COVID-19 vaccines and six cancer types, including breast, colorectal, gastric, lung, prostate and thyroid cancers, and a 27% higher overall cancer risk.

The review also cited a 2025 study of nearly 300,000 Italians that found cancer hospitalizations were moderately higher among COVID-19 vaccine recipients, with a particularly increased risk of bladder, breast and colorectal cancer.

The authors also cited a U.S. Armed Forces Health Surveillance Division report tracking non-Hodgkin lymphoma incidence among active-duty service members between 2017 and 2023. The report found a significant increase in some lymphomas in 2021, the year COVID-19 shots became widely available.

The authors said their findings “underscore the need for rigorous epidemiologic, longitudinal, clinical, histopathological, forensic, and mechanistic studies to assess whether and under what conditions COVID-19 vaccination or infection may be linked with cancer.”.

According to the review, a “growing number of peer-reviewed publications” have reported the appearance of diverse cancer types following COVID-19 vaccination or infection. My name is Delaina Hartman, and I am submitting this comment to share my personal medical experience following COVID-19 vaccination. I hope my experience contributes to discussions on vaccine safety monitoring and reported adverse events.

I am a police officer and first responder. During the COVID-19 pandemic I served my community on the front lines, interacting with the public daily. Like many frontline personnel, we were strongly encouraged to receive the COVID-19 vaccine and told it would help protect us while we carried out our duties.

I received my first two COVID-19 vaccine doses in good faith and had never contracted COVID-19 at that time. Shortly afterward, I began experiencing unexplained symptoms that significantly impacted my health and daily functioning.

Over time my condition progressed into what physicians later identified as dysautonomia and myalgic encephalomyelitis/chronic fatigue syndrome (ME/CFS). My illness has included severe fatigue, autonomic dysfunction, and significant blood glucose instability. During one episode my blood glucose dropped to 14 mg/dL, triggering a seizure while I was being transported to the ICU.

These health changes have significantly altered my life and made it difficult to continue in the profession I love. Despite these challenges, I continue working to remain a police officer.

I am sharing my experience not out of anger, but out of concern.

Across the country, individuals have reported symptoms following vaccination including autonomic dysfunction, neurological complications, and chronic fatigue-like syndromes. Many patients report difficulty obtaining recognition of their symptoms or finding physicians familiar with these conditions.

Patients living with these complications often struggle not only with complex medical symptoms but also with reporting systems and accessing appropriate care.

I respectfully ask that continued attention be given to: research into post-vaccine adverse events including dysautonomia and autonomic nervous system dysfunction; improved transparency and reporting within vaccine adverse event monitoring systems; support for clinicians researching and treating complex post-immune conditions; resources for patients experiencing serious long-term complications following vaccination; and consideration of policies related to manufacturer liability protections and patient recourse when harm occurs.

For patients whose lives have been altered by these conditions, this issue is not abstract—it is something we live with every day.

Public health trust depends on transparency, rigorous investigation of adverse events, and compassion for those experiencing complications.

To clarify the sequence of events, I have included the following timeline.

January–February 2021 — COVID-19 vaccination
I received my first two COVID-19 vaccine doses while serving as a frontline police officer during the pandemic. At that time I had never contracted COVID-19.

Post-vaccination symptom onset (2021)
Shortly after vaccination I developed multiple unexplained symptoms including severe fatigue, heart palpitations, muscle fatigue and weakness, muscle twitching, brain fog, peripheral neuropathy, gastrointestinal symptoms, muscle and joint pain, shortness of breath, sleep disturbances, visual disturbances, nausea, ringing in the ears (tinnitus), and chest pain. These symptoms significantly affected daily functioning and initially remained unexplained.

February 2022 — First COVID-19 infection
Approximately one year after symptom onset I contracted COVID-19. Symptoms worsened afterward.

March 2023 — Second COVID-19 infection
Following a second infection my condition deteriorated further.

My symptoms progressed into dysautonomia and ME/CFS with severe post-exertional malaise, leading to a significant decline in physical functioning.

Hospitalization for post-exertional malaise
I was hospitalized for four days and temporarily lost the ability to walk independently for approximately two weeks.

Hospitalization for hypoglycemia and seizures
About two and a half months later I experienced severe hypoglycemia and seizures requiring hospitalization and approximately four months off work.

Following further medical evaluation, physicians identified dysautonomia associated with Long COVID as the underlying condition.

Current status
I continue to live with dysautonomia and chronic fatigue-related symptoms while working to maintain my career as a police officer.

Thank you for the opportunity to submit my experience for the public record.

Respectfully,
Delaina Hartman

Jackson’s story
Diagnoses: Autism; Mercury-Poisoning; Heavy Metal Toxicity; Landau-Kleffner Syndrome (spike and wave in temporal lobe); Leaky Gut Syndrome; Thyroid Insufficiency; Sensory Integration Disorder; Auditory Processing Dysfunction; Tinnitus; Speech; Language and Social Delay; Pervasive Developmental Delay; Tourette’s Syndrome; Colitis; Attention Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder; Immune System Dysfunction causing food, inhalant and chemical allergies; Yeast and Viral overload,; High Measles Titer; Apraxia; Dyspraxia; Static Encephalopathy and Lymphoid Nodular Hyperplasia.

Jackson was born with 9 and 10 Apgar scores after receiving the best of prenatal care and delivery through the Fairfax, VA Hospital system. He met all developmental milestones until 16 months old. He had 25 words. Was putting two words together. He was answering simple questions. He had beginning pretending skills. He was a bright eyed, social baby and toddler who could tell the difference between the Concorde and a regular airplane and delighted in watching them from an airport near our home.

When Jackson was first diagnosed with Pervasive Developmental Disorder at 20 months old, he had just experienced a four month regression beginning days after his August 9, 1990 shots. He received HiB (with 25 mcg. of mercury) on July 25, 1990. Just two weeks later, on August 9, 1990, he received DT (with 25 mcg. of mercury), Oral Polio, and Measles-Mumps-Rubella. In the span of two weeks, he had received 50 mcg. of mercury, three dead viruses and four live viruses. The 50 mcg. of mercury was on top of the 75 mcg. of mercury he had already received in his first year of life through one DPT and two other DT shots. By design, the DT shots also carried extra aluminum. The 50 mcg. of mercury he received in one day was 44 times the EPA exposure limit for an adult. The total amount of mercury from 2 months old to 16 months old was 139 times the EPA exposure level for an adult.

The downfall in Jackson’s skills was swift and cruel. In the first two months after the shots, his eyes told us he was scared of what was happening to him. Rashes on his body would come and go. High spiking fevers would disappear as quickly as their onset. He would be surprised and cry as projectile vomiting seemed to also have sudden onset. Constipation, then diarrhea ravaged his body. Clearly his immune system was unraveling. He also asked us “why” with his eyes. He lost his language skills. While he could still understand language, he was no longer speaking. After two more months, his eyes neither asked nor told us anything. Like him, his eyes were totally void of any communication. His expressive and receptive language skills were completely gone. He no longer saw airplanes in the sky even though we pointed them out.

My outgoing, social, verbal child was a shell of his old self. Jackson’s body was still here. But his personality – what made Jackson a smart, loving baby and toddler – was gone. The sick body replaced the well one. The sleeping child was replaced by one who would wake startled after a few hours and not go back to sleep all night. The one who had a healthy appetite was replaced by one who became picky and whittled his foods down to one or two. The happy child was replaced by an unhappy one. Public Comment to the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP)
Date: March 5, 2026
Docket No.: CDC‑2026‑0199

Subject: Urgent Call for Immediate Suspension and Recall of COVID‑19 mRNA Products Pending Comprehensive Independent Review

Members of the Committee,
The continued authorization and distribution of the COVID‑19 mRNA products represent a profound breach of biomedical ethics, scientific integrity, and regulatory oversight. Mounting molecular and clinical evidence now demonstrates that these products pose risks incompatible with accepted standards of vaccine safety and public health protection.

I. Molecular and Biological Safety Concerns
1. DNA Contamination and Genomic Integration Risk
Independent analyses have documented the presence of residual DNA within the lipid nanoparticle formulations, including sequences containing the SV40 enhancer and promoter elements. The presence of these sequences introduces the potential for genomic incorporation and unintended, long‑term expression. This constitutes a serious manufacturing contamination event in violation of core quality‑control principles and existing FDA biologics standards.

2. Immune Tolerance and Class Switching
Observation of an IgG3 to IgG4 antibody class switch after injection indicates an atypical immune tolerance response. Rather than improving viral clearance, this pattern may dampen critical immune defenses and predispose recipients to reinfection or chronic viral persistence. The committee must formally assess whether continued boosting amplifies immune suppression or tolerance dysfunction.

3. Oncogenic Pathway Suppression
Experimental evidence demonstrates suppression of the p53 tumor‑suppressor pathway and disruption of DNA repair signaling following lipid nanoparticle exposure. Given the central role of p53 in preventing malignancy, any degree of sustained down‑regulation presents a plausible oncogenic risk that demands urgent investigation.

II. Regulatory and Ethical Misconduct

The development and Emergency Use Authorization of these products bypassed standard regulatory safeguards and long‑term toxicological testing routinely required for genetic biologics. The EUA framework was improperly leveraged to accelerate deployment without adequate biodistribution, mutagenesis, or reproductive safety assessments.

Simultaneously, the suppression of alternative therapeutics, censorship of clinicians, and retribution against dissenting physicians created conditions incompatible with the ethical principles enshrined in the Belmont Report—beneficence, respect for persons, and justice.

Mandates and coercion further violated the principle of informed consent, as defined in the Nuremberg Code and Declaration of Helsinki, by compelling individuals to submit under threat of economic or social exclusion.

III. Required Immediate Actions
Immediate Recall and Independent Lot Examination:
Suspend all distribution until a third‑party forensic analysis can examine manufacturing lots for DNA contamination, lipid nanoparticle degradation, and evidence of potential genomic integration.

Public Release of All Preclinical and Clinical Data:
Require full disclosure of genomic sequences, biodistribution findings, and internal safety evaluations currently withheld under proprietary exemptions.

Moratorium on Coercive Policies:
Prohibit all remaining mandates related to these products in employment, education, and medical settings.

Formation of a Restorative Bioethics Oversight Board:
Establish an independent panel composed of molecular biologists, immunologists, toxicologists, pathologists, and ethicists with no financial conflicts of interest to conduct a comprehensive reevaluation of product safety and regulatory integrity.

IV. Conclusion
The continued distribution of these mRNA products despite unresolved contamination, immunologic, and oncogenic concerns represents a historic failure of regulatory accountability. The ACIP now faces a defining choice: to perpetuate concealment or to initiate transparent reform that restores confidence in public health.

An immediate recall, full public disclosure, and independent investigation are essential to protect the public and reestablish the ethical foundation of modern medicine.

I am submitting this comment to document a serious gap in transparency, notification, and accountability when serious adverse events occur following vaccination.

When my son was three months old, he received the rotavirus vaccine Rotashield. Shortly afterward he developed intussusception, a life-threatening condition in which the intestine telescopes into itself. He required emergency surgery to save his life. Two months later he required a second emergency surgery due to severe adhesions from the first operation.

At the time, our family was never informed that intussusception had been associated with the vaccine. We were left believing this was an unexplained medical emergency.

Years later I learned that Rotashield had been withdrawn from the market after it was linked to an increased risk of intussusception. By the time I discovered this connection, my son was eighteen years old and the statute of limitations for filing under the National Vaccine Injury Compensation Program had long expired. Our family was denied even the opportunity to have the case reviewed.

This experience exposes a serious structural problem. When automobile manufacturers discover that a defect can harm consumers, they are required to issue recalls and notify owners. Families deserve the same level of transparency when a medical product administered to infants is later linked to serious harm.

The current system also raises a deeper ethical concern. Medical interventions must be grounded in informed consent. Policies that effectively pressure or require people to accept medical products while shielding manufacturers from direct liability undermine public trust. Forced medicine is an abomination to the principles of informed consent and bodily autonomy that should guide ethical medical policy.

I urge the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices to consider the following reforms:

• Establish clear mechanisms to notify families when significant vaccine safety signals emerge after a product is licensed or withdrawn.
• Reevaluate statute-of-limitations rules within the National Vaccine Injury Compensation Program when adverse events are discovered years later.
• Strengthen transparency and communication with families when serious adverse events are identified.
• Ensure policies governing liability and compensation treat affected families fairly.

No parent should discover decades later that a life-threatening medical event affecting their infant may have been connected to a product they were never warned about, only to learn that the system offers no path for acknowledgment or review.

Sincerely,
Rachael DiRenna

I am Amanda Miller, RN BSN CPN writing in regards to Docket CDC-2026-1099.
As a Certified Pediatric Nurse, my primary responsibility is the safety and well-being of children. Vaccination remains one of the most effective tools we have to protect pediatric patients from serious illness, hospitalization, disability, and death. In my clinical experience, routine immunizations are a critical component of preventive care and a cornerstone of modern public health.

Because children rely on adults, clinicians, and policymakers to safeguard their health, it is essential that access to recommended vaccines remains broad, equitable, and guided by strong scientific evidence. I strongly oppose efforts that would unnecessarily limit access to vaccines or weaken existing vaccine recommendations for the general public. Reducing access or narrowing recommendations risks increasing preventable disease, particularly among vulnerable children who depend on community immunity and timely immunization to stay healthy.

At the same time, maintaining public trust in vaccines requires transparency and robust safety monitoring. While vaccine injuries are rare, they can occur. Programs such as the Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System (VAERS) and the National Vaccine Injury Compensation Program (NVICP) are vital components of the vaccine safety infrastructure. These systems allow clinicians and patients to report adverse events, support ongoing safety surveillance, and provide a pathway for individuals who believe they have experienced vaccine-related harm to seek review and compensation.

Protecting public health means holding two truths at once: vaccines save lives and are overwhelmingly safe, and the small number of individuals who experience adverse events deserve to be heard, studied, and supported. Strengthening reporting systems, improving clinician education on adverse event reporting, and ensuring fair access to compensation programs are essential to maintaining confidence in vaccination programs.

I encourage the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP) to continue using transparent, evidence-based methodologies when evaluating vaccine data and making recommendations. Pediatric patients benefit most when vaccine guidance is clear, consistent, and grounded in rigorous scientific review.

Children deserve the strongest protection we can provide. Preserving broad access to recommended vaccines, maintaining rigorous safety monitoring, and supporting those who experience rare adverse outcomes together form the foundation of a trustworthy and effective immunization program.

I am writing to make the committee aware of a recent survey:
Health Freedom Defense Fund and Brownstone Institute initiated such a poll to find out. This Feb. 26-27 poll, conducted by Zogby Strategies, has documented remarkable supermajorities in favor of medical and health freedom, with numbers on objective questions exceeding 80%.

Polled were 1,000 registered voters with 93.6% definitely or very likely to vote. The party breakdown is 37% Republican, 36% Democrat, 27% Independent. Party breakdown shows broad support. The margin of error for overall results is +/- 3.2 percentage points.
Strongest areas of agreement (broad majority support):

Right to refuse medical treatment generally: 87.9% agree (58.8% strongly).
Right to make one’s own medical choices as a basic human right protected by law: 87.2% agree (59.5% strongly).
Doctors should discuss vaccine concerns openly without fear of medical board backlash: 88.1% agree (64.5% strongly — one of the highest “strongly agree” levels in the survey).
Health insurance should cover chosen treatments, including holistic/alternative options: 76.1% agree (43.6% strongly).
Right to refuse vaccines for adults: 80.4% agree (50.5% strongly).
Personal medical/vaccine decisions should never lead to employment denial: 70.6% agree (47.3% strongly).
Parents’ right to refuse vaccines for children/dependents: 65.7% agree — still a clear majority, but softer than adult refusal (37.4% strongly agree vs. 50.5% for adults).
On matters of school vaccine mandates, results show majorities:

Parents should be able to opt children out of school vaccine mandates: 54.5% agree (31.0% strongly). Among parents with children under 17, the agreement was 66.7%, with 42.8% strongly agreeing. To put this staggering result in context, other polls in recent years have concluded that more than 70% of the public supports school vaccine mandates.
College students should not have been expelled for refusing the COVID-19 vaccine: 65.4% agree (44.4% strongly).
On matters related to the COVID-19 era, the poll documents a strong majority opposed measures in retrospect:

COVID-19 lockdowns/restrictions caused excessive damage to American society: 61.9% agree (35.0% strongly) vs. 32.0% disagree.
On other matters related to medical freedom:

Childhood vaccine schedule expansion likely contributed to the rise in chronic diseases (among other factors): 48.3% agree vs. 38.2% disagree + 13.6% undecided — essentially split, but less than a decade ago, a strong majority said the vaccine schedule is safe, as noted in a Pew Research poll.
The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services’ decision to conduct additional vaccine safety research is justified: 68.6% agree vs. ~21% disagree + 10% undecided.
Investigating the effects of thimerosal (a mercury-based compound), aluminum, polysorbate-80, polyethylene glycol and formaldehyde used in everyday medical products; 77.8% support, 47.8% strongly. (This question concerns the ingredients in vaccines without mentioning the word vaccines, yielding even stronger support.)
Overall, the poll shows very strong support (80–88%) for adult medical autonomy, the right to refuse treatment/vaccines as adults, freedom of medical speech for doctors and protection from employment discrimination based on medical choices.

Majority support remains when the question involves children (school mandates 54-66%, parental refusal for dependents 66%). More noteworthy, however, these results illustrate erosion in public support for school vaccine mandates since 2019, as also seen in other surveys.

Retrospective judgment on COVID-19 policies leans toward viewing them as excessively damaging. Trust in figures like Dr. Anthony Fauci and associated public health directives remains low (around 28-35%), with majorities believing guidance prioritized other interests or enabled excessive restrictions.

There is also broad approval for more vaccine safety research. The results reflect an electorate (especially among likely 2028 voters) that is protective of individual medical decision-making rights.

Finally, the poll results demonstrate that Americans will vote for candidates who protect their rights and freedoms, insist on transparency, and hold experts, pharmaceutical, and chemical companies accountable for their actions.

The lessons of this poll are palpable. If people are asked straightforward questions that impact their own health and medical wishes, and those of their families, they clearly come down on the side of freedom, transparency, honesty, and choice. This should not be surprising because these are core American values on which people are more united than divided.

I am submitting this comment to share concerns about informed consent practices and access to pediatric care related to vaccination policies.

A close friend of mine experienced a situation where their child was born healthy and was already walking at around 12 months of age. After receiving a vaccination following a recent flu illness, the child reportedly experienced significant developmental regression, becoming non-verbal and losing the ability to walk. According to the doctors involved, administering the vaccine shortly after a flu illness may have contributed to the outcome.

Experiences like this have raised concerns for me about how informed consent is handled in medical offices. In my experience, many doctors spend significant time explaining the risks of not vaccinating but do not spend equal time explaining potential side effects or screening for recent illnesses and allergies before administering vaccines. Parents should receive balanced information about both the benefits and the possible risks so they can make informed decisions for their children.

Doctors should consistently ask detailed questions about recent illnesses, immune reactions, and allergies before administering vaccines. Clearer screening procedures and transparent communication could help reduce potential complications and increase trust between parents and healthcare providers.

Another concern relates to access to pediatric care in Texas. In my experience, almost all pediatric offices make it extremely difficult for families who delay or decline vaccines to access routine care for their children. Finding a pediatrician willing to see children who are not fully vaccinated can feel like searching for a needle in a haystack. In some cases, offices refuse to schedule well-child visits for children who are not sick if they are not fully vaccinated.

If medical offices are concerned about exposure risks, a reasonable solution could be designated appointment hours or separate scheduling blocks rather than denying preventive care altogether. Children should still have access to routine pediatric care, regardless of vaccination status.

Parents should be able to openly discuss vaccination decisions with their pediatricians and still maintain access to routine healthcare for their children. Policies should encourage transparent communication, stronger informed consent practices, and continued access to care for all children.

Thank you for considering these concerns when evaluating vaccination policies and healthcare access regulations.

Vaccines, like any medical intervention, are not entirely without risk. While serious adverse reactions appear to be rare, they do occur, and it is important that these events are studied carefully and transparently. One of the ongoing challenges in medicine is understanding why some individuals may experience significant reactions while many others do not. It is important that we hear the stories of these people who have been vaccine-injured and find a way to help them.

As we continue to collect long-term data, it will be important for researchers and healthcare professionals to monitor potential patterns related to cardiovascular, neurological, and immune health over time. Ongoing research and open scientific dialogue are essential to maintaining both safety and public trust.

I also know someone personally who lost their mother shortly after receiving her second Moderna dose. Within a few days, she became critically ill and passed away from a blood clot in the brain. Whether or not the vaccine directly caused the outcome, it was a tragic situation that left many questions for the family. Experiences like this remind us how important compassionate listening, transparency, and continued research are in medicine.

Rather than dismissing concerns outright, we should be willing to ask thoughtful questions and continue learning.

How can the medical community better identify why certain individuals may experience severe reactions to medical treatments while others do not?

What systems are currently in place to track long-term outcomes after new medical interventions, and how can those systems be improved?

How do we balance public health recommendations with individualized medical risk assessment?

What role does informed consent play when recommending treatments that are still being studied long-term?

How can healthcare providers maintain public trust while still acknowledging uncertainty in emerging science?

Questions to consider moving forward.

My brother was diagnosed with T‑cell lymphoma just a few months after receiving the Moderna COVID shots in March 2021. He died only five months later, following a nightmarish ordeal of treatment at an overwhelmed hospital, leaving our family in deep grief.
(His case has been submitted to VAERS, CICP, and Moderna, and supporting documentation is available upon request.)

In the years following the COVID vaccination rollout—especially throughout 2022 and 2023—I witnessed an alarming surge in serious health crises among people I know who received these shots. These included sudden and aggressive cancers—lymphoma, throat cancer, and even liver cancer in a young person—along with heart attacks and aneurysms.

Alongside these tragedies came a marked increase in autoimmune and inflammatory disorders: POTS, eczema, celiac disease, visual disturbances, endocrine dysfunction, dysautonomia, severe allergic reactions, and exacerbations of inflammatory bowel diseases. Many of these conditions appeared suddenly in young, previously healthy individuals following vaccination—often after misleading information, coercion, or pressure from workplace and university mandates.

As a chemist, I independently reviewed the official CDC and FDA data in 2021. It quickly became apparent that, particularly for younger demographics, the risks of vaccination far outweighed any potential benefits. With the court‑ordered release of manufacturer trial data and the emergence of new variants, the picture became undeniable: no meaningful benefit—only harm. The vaccines disrupted immune function, increased susceptibility to infection, and failed to prevent transmission—an admission confirmed under oath by manufacturer executives in the European Parliament.

We now face a profound medical and ethical crisis—one defined not by a virus, but by widespread institutional denial. Those harmed continue to suffer while silence and deflection replace accountability.

The question before you is simple yet profound:
Will you continue hiding behind reassuring language like “shared decision‑making” and “protecting the vulnerable” when the immunocompromised are precisely those most at risk from these products?
Will institutions continue to impose these experimental injections on healthy individuals?
Will you allow these shots upon innocent children whose only misfortune is having parents misled by relentless propaganda?

Or will you finally confront the truth—acknowledge the injured and the bereaved, ban these harmful injections outright, remove them from the market, and launch a nationwide effort to treat the injured and educate those deceived by the false promise of “safe and effective”?

Only through such decisive action can this tragedy be addressed, healing begin, and integrity be restored to medicine and public health.

Thank you.
